alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine) and process hygiene criteria
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance with Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 required. This regulation defines the acceptability of a product or a batch of food and lays down process hygiene criteria to indicate the correct functioning of the production process.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This regulation sets out the general requirements for labelling of prepackaged food.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - Halal food general requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This regulation outlines the general requirements for Halal food.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - Mandatory expiration dates
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This regulation specifies mandatory expiration dates for food products.

alerts.title: Added: India Allergen List - Mandatory allergen declarations
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific requirements for allergen declaration on food labels for all food products, including seasoning and snack products, are detailed in the referenced compendium. Manufacturers must consult the full document for compliance.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Additive Limits - Use of additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Only MHLW-specified safe additives allowed. Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives) and food containing them are prohibited for sale, production, import, etc., unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies them as unlikely to cause harm to human health.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Contamination, foreign substances, or other grounds causing harm
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not allowed. General prohibition for food likely to cause harm to human health.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Diseases, abnormalities, or death
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not allowed for sale. Prohibition of selling meat, bones, milk, organs, and blood from livestock or poultry that have or are suspected to have specified diseases or abnormalities, or have died, except for cases specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, veterinary drugs
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must not exceed MHLW-specified safe quantities. Food containing residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or pharmaceutical products for animals in quantities exceeding MHLW-specified safe limits is prohibited. Verification of specific MHLW limits is required.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Toxic or harmful substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not allowed (unless specified safe by MHLW). Prohibition of food or additives containing or suspected to contain toxic or harmful substances, unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies them as unlikely to cause harm.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Notification of harm from designated ingredients
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory notification. Businesses handling food with MHLW-designated ingredients must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. without delay if the food causes or may cause harm to human health.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Microbiology Limits - Pathogens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not allowed if likely to cause harm. Prohibition of food contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, if likely to cause harm to human health.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Non-permitted Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from import or sale. An application can be submitted to SFA for evaluation of non-permitted additives.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (incidental constituents)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits. All food businesses must comply.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants exceeding regulatory limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from import or sale.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Diethylstibestrol, Hexoestrol, Dienoestrol residues
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Prohibition under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.

alerts.title: Modified: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Ingredients and Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Must be declared on food labels. New: Must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels. Applies to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Presence as an ingredient or food additive. Labeling is required if these are present as ingredients or food additives.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Sulphite (as residue sulphur dioxide)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 10. Labeling is required if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Prohibited Food Products
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Certain food products are prohibited from production, import, or sale in Thailand.

alerts.title: Added: United States Additive Limits - Prohibited Substances and Delisted Color Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) and delisted color additives in 21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30.

alerts.title: Added: United States Allergen List - Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Declaration Required. The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. All FDA requirements applicable to major food allergens, including labeling and manufacturing requirements, apply to sesame as of January 1, 2023.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 1. Guidance Level for 3-MCPD in acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. Action Level for total aflatoxins in foods, peanuts and peanut products, pistachio nuts, and Brazil nuts.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Chemical Contaminants (Tolerances, Action Levels, Guidance Levels)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification Required. Contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances are published in various guidance documents and regulations (21 CFR parts 109 and 509). The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Allergen Preventive Controls
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. The CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. Facilities must put written procedures in place.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Cochineal Extract and Carmine
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Declaration Required. Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - FD&C Yellow No. 5
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Declaration Required. All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Gluten-Free Claim
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Defined Standard. The FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling. Compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim were established on August 12, 2020.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Major Food Allergen Declaration
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. The food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name (e.g., 'lecithin (soy)') OR immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a 'contains' statement (e.g., 'Contains wheat, milk, and soy.'). The specific type of tree nut, species of fish, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Sulfiting Agents
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: ≥10. Various sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: Old: Check official source. New: Verification required. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth conditions for permitted food additives. Specific provisions are available in the searchable online database.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Maximum levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food. These are detailed in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and other related texts.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
alerts.details: Old: Check official source. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives and their conditions of use
alerts.details: New: Verification required against the Union list of food additives in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008. The database serves as a tool to inform about approved food additives and their conditions of use in the EU.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Union list of food additives in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria, toxins and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Data label requirements for packaged products
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This standard outlines general requirements for data labels on packaged products, applicable to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This regulation outlines general labelling requirements for all prepackaged foodstuffs, which would include allergen declarations, ingredient lists, and potentially warnings relevant to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Mandatory shelf life periods
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for food products, which must be declared on the label for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Optional shelf life periods
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This regulation provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods for food products, which may be declared on the label for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Quantity declaration in prepackaged packages
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This standard relates to the accurate declaration of product quantity on labels for prepackaged products, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Labelling Requirement - General requirements for Halal food
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Labelling Requirement - Labelling requirements for prepackaged food
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Labelling Requirement - Mandatory expiration dates for food products
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Labelling Requirement - Voluntary expiration dates for food products
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological limits
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation specifies microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological limits
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: India Additive Limits - Permitted food additives and their maximum limits
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific permitted food additives and their maximum limits for various food categories, including seasoning and snack products, are detailed in the referenced compendium. Manufacturers must consult the full document for compliance.

alerts.title: Added: India Labelling Requirement - General labelling and display requirements
alerts.details: New: Verification required. General labelling and display requirements for food products, including seasoning and snack products, are detailed in the referenced compendium. This includes aspects like nutritional information, ingredient list, net quantity, etc. Manufacturers must consult the full document for compliance.

alerts.title: Added: India Salt / Sodium Limits - Declaration of salt/sodium content and related claims
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Requirements for the declaration of salt/sodium content on food labels for products, including seasoning and snack products, and conditions for making claims related to salt/sodium content, are detailed in the referenced compendium. Manufacturers must consult the full document for compliance.

alerts.title: Modified: Indonesia Additive Limits - Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. New: Verification required. This circular letter specifies provisions for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive. Actual limits and conditions must be verified from the full document.

alerts.title: Added: Indonesia Labelling Requirement - Food Category Definition
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This regulation defines food categories, which is essential for understanding the scope of other food regulations, proper product classification, and related labelling requirements. The full document must be consulted.

alerts.title: Added: Indonesia Labelling Requirement - General Compliance
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific regulations for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be consulted in the BPOM legal database. There are 281 regulations listed under 'Pangan Olahan'.

alerts.title: Removed: Indonesia Labelling Requirement - Export Certificate Procedures
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Indonesia Labelling Requirement - Food Categorization
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Indonesia Labelling Requirement - Oversight of Online Distributed Food
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Agricultural Chemical Residues
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Food (general)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Certification of sanitation
alerts.details: New: Required. Imported livestock/poultry meat, milk, organs, and products thereof must have certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization, confirming they are free from specified diseases/abnormalities.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Certification of sanitation management
alerts.details: New: Required. Food and additives specified by MHLW as requiring certification of production site sanitation management must not be imported for sale unless accompanied by certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Production, processing, use, cooking, preservation methods, and ingredients
alerts.details: New: Must conform to MHLW-established criteria/standards. Food or additives must conform to criteria established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare for methods of production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation, and to standards for ingredients. Verification of specific MHLW criteria and standards is required.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Production/processing in MHLW-specified facilities/regions
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Food and additives specified by MHLW as requiring important process control measures must only be imported if produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries, regions, or facilities that implement those measures.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Labelling Requirement - General Food Safety Compliance
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Middle East (GCC/GSO) Salt / Sodium Limits - Declaration of salt/sodium content in prepackaged foodstuffs

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II Chemical Preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
alerts.details: New: Permitted levels must be calculated using SFA's Calculation Tool if two or more are used. This applies when two or more Class II chemical preservatives are used in the food.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required for specific limits. Limits depend on the additive and food type. Refer to 'Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives'.

alerts.title: Removed: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II chemical preservatives
alerts.details: Old: Within prescribed regulatory limits. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
alerts.details: Old: Not to be directly added during manufacturing. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
